citRespParty.rpOrgName: Alaska Department of Fish and Game

dataIdInfo.idAbs:
This metadata report applies to the 2018 ADF&G Salmon and Miscellaneous Finfish Statistical Areas. Statistical areas are polygons that divide the waters of the State of Alaska and the adjacent Exclusive Economic Zone (EEZ) into small units for the purpose of reporting and analyzing fishery harvest. Each statistical area is identified by a unique 5-digit number. Salmon statistical areas can be divided by lines defined in ADF&G regulations, and further subdivided based on prominent landmarks or points of land. All regulatory lines were originally referenced to the NAD83 datum and later projected to the WGS84 datum in this feature class. This GIS version of the salmon statistical areas effective January 1, 2017, although this rendition of statistical areas became effective in 2018. If you need to compare to current statistical areas, you will need to refer to other versions of the salmon statistical areas.

This project received funding under award NA15NMF4270272 from NOAA Fisheries Service, in cooperation with the Saltonstall-Kennedy Grant Program.

The statements, findings, conclusions, and recommendations are those of the author(s) and do not necessarily reflect the views of NOAA Fisheries. THESTATE OF ALASKA, DEPARTMENT OF FISH AND GAME, MAKES NO EXPRESS OR IMPLIED WARRANTIES (INCLUDING WARRANTIES OF MERCHANTABILITY AND FITNESS) WITH RESPECT TO THE ACCURACY, CHARACTER, FUNCTION, OR CAPABILITIES OF THE DATA, SERVICES, OR PRODUCTS OR THEIR APPROPRIATENESS FOR ANY PARTICULAR PURPOSE. The Department of Fish and Game is not liable for any direct, incidental, indirect, special, compensatory, consequential or other damages suffered by the user or any other person or entity from the use of or failure of the data, services, or products even if the Department of Fish and Game has been advised of the possibility of such potential loss or damage. The entire risk as to the results of the use of the data, services, or products is assumed by the user. Datamay be subject to periodic change without prior notification. To ensure receipt of the most current information, please refer requests to the ADF&G Open Data website. Users may not reproduce or distribute these data without explicit consent of the Alaska Department of Fish and Game. Thesedata are provided for general visual reference and to aid users in generating various natural resource analyses and products. There are no constraints to accessing these data, other than the limitations or constraints associated with a specific dataset or set forth herein. It is strongly recommended that careful attention be paid to the contents of the metadata file associated with these data. Anyhardcopy or electronic products utilizing these data shall clearly indicate their source. If the user has modified the data in any way, the user must describe the modifications they have performed. The user agrees not to misrepresent these data, nor to imply that the Alaska Department of Fish and Game approved any changes they made.
dataIdInfo.idPurp:
Participants in Alaska commercial fisheries are required to report the statistical area where their harvest occurs. In combination with reports on the harvest species, poundage and etc., these data are useful to analyze and visualize spatial patterns in harvest. This data set defines the current valid statistical areas that may be used on ADF&G fish tickets for salmon and miscellaneous finfish. This data set should NOT be used for navigation or for determining compliance with ADF&G Commercial Fishing Regulations. Please consult the ADF&G Commercial Fisheries Regulations for the official definitions of regulatory boundaries.
NOTE: These data attempt to depict statistical area boundaries as used for management during a specific time period. In some cases, statistical area boundaries used in practice may differ from those described in regulations, reports, maps, and other aids.
dataIdInfo.idCredit: Alaska Department of Fish and Game, Commercial Fisheries Division

searchKeys.keyword: Alaska Department of Fish and Game

dataExt.exDesc: These data include statistical areas from January 1, 1961

idPoC.rpOrgName: Alaska Department of Fish and Game

scpExt.exDesc: This data quality report applies to the commercial salmon statistical area polygon features and associated attributes published in the PVB_PWS_2018 feature class.

dataLineage.statement: The origin of this dataset is PVB_Statewide_2007 (originally named "SALMON_32606"). Source data was originated by Evelyn Russel in 2006 (ADF&G) and was never published.

prcStep.stepDesc: Polygons representing the Prince William Sound Area were exported and the feature class was named "PVB_PWS_1961". Attribute data were edited accordingly. (S. Larsen)
prcStep.stepRat: The intention of this step was to isolate the statistical area polygons that represent the Prince William Sound Area in 1961.

prcStep.stepDesc: PVB_PWS_1961 was exported and renamed PVB_PWS_1962. Polygons were aligned to the regulatory coordiantes used in 1962 and all attribute data were updated (S.Larsen).
prcStep.stepRat: The intention of this step was to isolate the statistical area polygons that represent the Prince William Sound Area in 1962.

prcStep.stepDesc: PVB_PWS_1962 was exported and renamed PVB_PWS_1975. Polygons were aligned to the regulatory coordiantes used in 1975 and all attribute data were updated (S.Larsen).
prcStep.stepRat: The intention of this step was to isolate the statistical area polygons that represent the Prince William Sound Area in 1975.

prcStep.stepDesc: PVB_PWS_1975 was exported and renamed PVB_PWS_1976. Polygons were aligned to the regulatory coordiantes used in 1976 and all attribute data were updated (S.Larsen).
prcStep.stepRat: The intention of this step was to isolate the statistical area polygons that represent the Prince William Sound Area in 1976.

prcStep.stepDesc: PVB_PWS_1976 was exported and renamed PVB_PWS_1977. Polygons were aligned to the regulatory coordiantes used in 1977 and all attribute data were updated (S.Larsen).
prcStep.stepRat: The intention of this step was to isolate the statistical area polygons that represent the Prince William Sound Area in 1977.

prcStep.stepDesc: PVB_PWS_1977 was exported and renamed PVB_PWS_1979. Polygons were aligned to the regulatory coordiantes used in 1979 and all attribute data were updated (S.Larsen).
prcStep.stepRat: The intention of this step was to isolate the statistical area polygons that represent the Prince William Sound Area in 1979.

prcStep.stepDesc: PVB_PWS_1979 was exported and renamed PVB_PWS_1982. Polygons were aligned to the regulatory coordiantes used in 1982 and all attribute data were updated (S.Larsen).
prcStep.stepRat: The intention of this step was to isolate the statistical area polygons that represent the Prince William Sound Area in 1982.

prcStep.stepDesc: PVB_PWS_1982 was exported and renamed PVB_PWS_1983. Polygons were aligned to the regulatory coordiantes used in 1983 and all attribute data were updated (S.Larsen).
prcStep.stepRat: The intention of this step was to isolate the statistical area polygons that represent the Prince William Sound Area in 1983.

prcStep.stepDesc: PVB_PWS_1983 was exported and renamed PVB_PWS_1985. Polygons were aligned to the regulatory coordiantes used in 1985 and all attribute data were updated (S.Larsen).
prcStep.stepRat: The intention of this step was to isolate the statistical area polygons that represent the Prince William Sound Area in 1985.

prcStep.stepDesc: PVB_PWS_1985 was exported and renamed PVB_PWS_1987. Polygons were aligned to the regulatory coordiantes used in 1987 and all attribute data were updated (S.Larsen).
prcStep.stepRat: The intention of this step was to isolate the statistical area polygons that represent the Prince William Sound Area in 1987.

prcStep.stepDesc: PVB_PWS_1987 was exported and renamed PVB_PWS_1988. Polygons were aligned to the regulatory coordiantes used in 1988 and all attribute data were updated (S.Larsen).
prcStep.stepRat: The intention of this step was to isolate the statistical area polygons that represent the Prince William Sound Area in 1988.

prcStep.stepDesc: PVB_PWS_1988 was exported and renamed PVB_PWS_1990. Polygons were aligned to the regulatory coordiantes used in 1990 and all attribute data were updated (S.Larsen).
prcStep.stepRat: The intention of this step was to isolate the statistical area polygons that represent the Prince William Sound Area in 1990.

prcStep.stepDesc: PVB_PWS_1990 was exported and renamed PVB_PWS_1991. Polygons were aligned to the regulatory coordiantes used in 1991 and all attribute data were updated (S.Larsen).
prcStep.stepRat: The intention of this step was to isolate the statistical area polygons that represent the Prince William Sound Area in 1991.

prcStep.stepDesc: PVB_PWS_1991 was exported and renamed PVB_PWS_2003. Polygons were aligned to the regulatory coordiantes used in 2003 and all attribute data were updated (S.Larsen).
prcStep.stepRat: The intention of this step was to isolate the statistical area polygons that represent the Prince William Sound Area in 2003.

prcStep.stepDesc: PVB_PWS_2003 was exported and renamed PVB_PWS_2004. Polygons were aligned to the regulatory coordiantes used in 2004 and all attribute data were updated (S.Larsen).
prcStep.stepRat: The intention of this step was to isolate the statistical area polygons that represent the Prince William Sound Area in 2004.

prcStep.stepDesc: PVB_PWS_2004 was exported and renamed PVB_PWS_2006. Polygons were aligned to the regulatory coordiantes used in 2006 and all attribute data were updated (S.Larsen).
prcStep.stepRat: The intention of this step was to isolate the statistical area polygons that represent the Prince William Sound Area in 2006.

prcStep.stepDesc: PVB_PWS_2006 was exported and renamed PVB_PWS_2009. Polygons were aligned to the regulatory coordiantes used in 2009 and all attribute data were updated (S.Larsen).
prcStep.stepRat: The intention of this step was to isolate the statistical area polygons that represent the Prince William Sound Area in 2009.

prcStep.stepDesc: PVB_PWS_2009 was exported and renamed PVB_PWS_2012. Polygons were aligned to the regulatory coordiantes used in 2012 and all attribute data were updated (S.Larsen).
prcStep.stepRat: The intention of this step was to isolate the statistical area polygons that represent the Prince William Sound Area in 2012.

prcStep.stepDesc: PVB_PWS_2012 was exported and renamed PVB_PWS_2018. Polygons were aligned to the regulatory coordiantes used in 2018 and all attribute data were updated (S.Larsen).
prcStep.stepRat: The intention of this step was to isolate the statistical area polygons that represent the Prince William Sound Area in 2018.

dataSource.srcDesc: The source data for the coastline was "Alaska Coastline 1:63,360", published by the Alaska Department of Natural Resources, Land Records Section, on 02/11/1998.

report.measDesc: The Record Completeness measure is intended to ensure that all attribute records are complete. This measure does not include data correctness, only record completion.
report.evalMethDesc: Attribute records were examined for completeness using the "Select by Attribute" method. Unique values should consist of one of the values described under the Fields section of this metadata.

ConResult.conExpl: All attibute records are recorded as appropriate (03/21/2018 S.Larsen).

report.measDesc: The Record Accuracy measure is intended to ensure that all attribute records are accurate.
report.evalMethDesc: Attribute records were examined for accuracy by regional staff.

ConResult.conExpl: All attibute records are recorded correctly (J. Botz 03/21/2018).

report.measDesc: The Polygon Accuracy measure is intended to ensure that statistical area polygons are accurate.
report.evalMethDesc: Regulatory coordinates (e.g., Registration Areas, Districts, Subdistricts) were recorded from the appropriate Alaska Fish and Game Laws and Regulations, and plotted over the appropriate feature class.

enttyp.enttypd: Commercial Salmon Fishery Statistical Areas valid as of 1/1/2018
enttyp.enttypds: Alaska Department of Fish and Game

attr.attrdef: Internal feature number.
attr.attrdefs: Esri

attrdomv.udom: Sequential unique whole numbers that are automatically generated.

attr.attrdef: Feature geometry.
attr.attrdefs: Esri

attrdomv.udom: Coordinates defining the features.

attr.attrdef: Effective start date for a particular polygon. This field is intended to capture the effective start date for a given configuration of a statistical area.
attr.attrdefs: Alaska Department of Fish and Game

attrdomv.udom: Date corresponding to the day, month, and year that a particular configuration of a statistical area was established. Usually corresponds to January 1st.

attr.attrdef: Effective end date for a particular polygon. This field is intended to capture the effective end date for a given configuration of a statistical area.
attr.attrdefs: Alaska Department of Fish and Game

attrdomv.udom: Date corresponding to the day, month, and year that a particular configuration of a statistical area was retired. Usually corresponds to December 31st.

attr.attrdef: A statistical area indicates the physical location where a fishery harvest is made.
attr.attrdefs: Alaska Department of Fish and Game

edom.edomvds: Alaska Department of Fish and Game Commercial Fisheries Division

attr.attrdef: The name of a statistical area.
attr.attrdefs: Alaska Department of Fish and Game

attrdomv.udom: Unique text string associated with one statistical area number.

attr.attrdef: One of the four major fishery groups aggregated by species type.
attr.attrdefs: Alaska Department of Fish and Game

edom.edomvds: Alaska Department of Fish and Game

attr.attrdef: Name of the GIS series that a feature class belongs to.
attr.attrdefs: Alaska Department of Fish and Game

edom.edomvd: Feature classes associated with commercial salmon fisheries
edom.edomvds: Alaska Department of Fish and Game

attr.attrdef: One letter code that corresponds to name of the GIS series that a feature class belongs to.
attr.attrdefs: Alaska Department of Fish and Game

edom.edomvds: Alaska Department of Fish and Game

attr.attrdef: A term used by the ADF&G to divide the management of commercial fishery resources in Alaska. Regions are divided on a state level and are the highest division of management in ADF&G.
attr.attrdefs: Alaska Department of Fish and Game

edom.edomvds: Alaska Department of Fish and Game

attr.attrdef: The name of a physical location described in regulations. Corresponds to the location that a commercial fishing vessel is registered to fish in.
attr.attrdefs: Alaska Department of Fish and Game

attr.attrdef: A one letter code which refers to a physical area within an ADF&G Region. Registration Areas are used for commercial fishery management purposes.
attr.attrdefs: Alaska Department of Fish and Game

attr.attrdef: The name of a physical area used for management purposes. Management areas are established at the agency level.
attr.attrdefs: Alaska Department of Fish and Game

attrdomv.udom: There are no Management Areas included in this dataset

attr.attrdef: A series of letters used to refer to a Management Area.
attr.attrdefs: Alaska Department of Fish and Game

attrdomv.udom: There are no Management Areas included in this dataset

attr.attrdef: A physical area defined in regulations, used for commercial fishery management. Districts are located within Registration Areas.
attr.attrdefs: Alaska Department of Fish and Game

attr.attrdef: A code used to refer to a district.
attr.attrdefs: Alaska Department of Fish and Game

edom.edomvds: Alaska Department of Fish and Game

Alaska Department of Fish and Game
Alaska Department of Fish and Game
Alaska Department of Fish and Game
Alaska Department of Fish and Game
Alaska Department of Fish and Game
Alaska Department of Fish and Game
Alaska Department of Fish and Game

attr.attrdef: A physical area defined in regulations, used for commercial fishery management. Subdistricts are located within Districts.
attr.attrdefs: Alaska Department of Fish and Game

attr.attrdef: A series of letters used to refer to a subdistrict.
attr.attrdefs: Alaska Department of Fish and Game

attrdomv.udom: <Null> There are no Subdistrict Codes in this dataset.

attr.attrdef: A physical area defined in regulations, used for commercial fishery management. Sections are located within Districts or Subdistricts.
attr.attrdefs: Alaska Department of Fish and Game

attrdomv.udom: <Null> There are no Sections in this dataset.

attr.attrdef: A series of letters used to refer to a Section
attr.attrdefs: Alaska Department of Fish and Game

attrdomv.udom: <Null> There are no Sections in this dataset.

attr.attrdef: The name of the individual user who created this polygon
attr.attrdefs: Alaska Department of Fish and Game

attrdomv.udom: Text String

attr.attrdef: A physical area defined in regulations, used for commercial fishery management. Subsections are located within Sections.
attr.attrdefs: Alaska Department of Fish and Game

attrdomv.udom: <Null> There are no Subsections in this dataset.

attr.attrdef: A series of letters used to refer to a Subsection
attr.attrdefs: Alaska Department of Fish and Game

attrdomv.udom: <Null> There are no Subsections in this dataset.

attr.attrdef: A two digit code that represents Commercial Operating Annual Report area.
attr.attrdefs: Alaska Department of Fish and Game

edom.edomvds: Alaska Department of Fish and Game
Alaska Department of Fish and Game
Alaska Department of Fish and Game

attr.attrdef: Date a polygon feature was created.
attr.attrdefs: Alaska Department of Fish and Game

edom.edomvds: Alaska Department of Fish and Game

attr.attrdef: Name of the individual that edited a polygon feature
attr.attrdefs: Alaska Department of Fish and Game

attrdomv.udom: The name of an individual user.

attr.attrdef: Date a polygon feature was edited.
attr.attrdefs: Alaska Department of Fish and Game

attrdomv.udom: A calendar date.

attr.attrdef: Additional information about a feature's attributes.
attr.attrdefs: Alaska Department of Fish and Game

attrdomv.udom: Additional information about a feature's attributes.

attr.attrdef: Length of feature in internal units.
attr.attrdefs: Esri

attrdomv.udom: Positive real numbers that are automatically generated.

attr.attrdef: Area of feature in internal units squared.
attr.attrdefs: Esri

attrdomv.udom: Positive real numbers that are automatically generated.

attr.attrdef: The name of the applicable fishery stock used for management purposes. Fishery stock boundaries are derived using various biological factors and are used for management purposes. Stock boundaries are not always defined in ADF&G Regulations.
attr.attrdefs: Alaska Department of Fish and Game

attrdomv.udom: There are no Fishery Stocks included in this dataset.

attr.attrdef: A code used to refer to a specific Fishery Stock.
attr.attrdefs: Alaska Department of Fish and Game

attrdomv.udom: A code used to refer to a specific Fishery Stock.

mdContact.rpOrgName: Alaska Department of Fish and Game
